title: The Loneliness of Big Cities
content: When Lily first moved to the city, she imagined constant excitement, opportunity, and connection. The streets were always crowded, cafés remained open late into the night, and thousands of people passed beside her every day. Yet after several months, she began noticing an unexpected loneliness hidden beneath the movement and noise. Most interactions felt brief and superficial. Neighbors rarely spoke, commuters avoided eye contact, and friendships often disappeared beneath busy schedules. One evening while walking home through crowded sidewalks, Lily realized she had never felt more anonymous in her life. The city offered endless activity, yet genuine connection still seemed surprisingly difficult to find within it.
